info.title: Aardvark Gateway API
info.description:
User-facing HTTP API of the aardvark cloud gateway — the metered OpenRouter proxy and dashboard backend. Operator-only endpoints (`/admin/*`, `/operator-auth/*`, `/operator-api/*`) and the dashboard / operator SPA shells (`/dashboard*`, `/operator*`) are excluded by design.

This document is GENERATED from the gateway source: `scripts/gen-openapi.mjs` extracts every route's auth, request fields, query params, response shape, and error codes directly from the handlers, then renders them. Do NOT edit `openapi.json` (or hand-author its structure) — change the code, and regenerate. Only prose lives in `scripts/openapi/descriptions.mjs`.

get.description: Unauthenticated root health check; returns a fixed `text/plain` body.

post.x-aardvark-browser-tryit-reason: This auth endpoint is same-origin to the gateway dashboard, so browser calls from this docs page are blocked by CORS. Use a generated request sample or the dashboard instead.
post.description: Deletes the session row and clears the cookie. Requires the session cookie and the `X-Aardvark-Dashboard` CSRF header. Idempotent.

ok.description: Always `true`; the session row was deleted and the `__Host-av_session` cookie cleared.

error.message: Could not complete sign-out. Please try again.

get.x-aardvark-browser-tryit-reason: This auth endpoint is same-origin to the gateway dashboard, so browser calls from this docs page are blocked by CORS. Use a generated request sample or the dashboard instead.
get.description: Returns the authenticated user, the active account, the role there, and all memberships. Returns 401 `{ authenticated: false }` when there is no valid session.

authenticated.description: `true` for a valid session; the 401 body instead carries `false`.

id.description: Stable user id of the signed-in account holder.

email.description: Login email of the signed-in user.

user.description: The signed-in user identity (id and login email).

active_account.description: Account id the session is currently scoped to for billing and `/v1/*` access.

role.description: Caller's role in `active_account`: one of `owner`, `admin`, or `member`.
User's role on that account: `owner`, `admin`, or `member`.

owner_email.description: Owner email of that account, shown to identify it in the switcher.

memberships.description: All accounts the user is an active member of, for the account switcher.

post.x-aardvark-browser-tryit-reason: This auth endpoint is same-origin to the gateway dashboard, so browser calls from this docs page are blocked by CORS. Use a generated request sample or the dashboard instead.
post.description: Emails a single-use magic link to the address. Neutral 200 regardless of whether the email exists (no account enumeration). Rate limited per IP and per email.

email.description: Email to send the sign-in link to.

ok.description: Always `true`; the response is neutral and never signals whether the email exists.

message.description: Neutral confirmation prose shown regardless of outcome, avoiding account enumeration.

error.message: Too many sign-in requests. Please wait a minute and try again.

post.x-aardvark-browser-tryit-reason: This auth endpoint is same-origin to the gateway dashboard, so browser calls from this docs page are blocked by CORS. Use a generated request sample or the dashboard instead.
post.description: Rotates the session to a different account the user belongs to. Requires the session cookie and the `X-Aardvark-Dashboard` CSRF header. Rate limited per user.

account_id.description: Target account id to switch to.

ok.description: Always `true` once the session is rotated to the target account.

active_account.description: Account id the session is now active on, matching the requested `account_id`.

error.message: Too many account switches. Please wait a moment.

get.x-aardvark-browser-tryit-reason: This endpoint serves the magic-link verification HTML page, not a cross-origin JSON API request. Open the magic link in your browser or start the flow from the dashboard instead.
get.description: Serves a tiny nonce-CSP HTML page that reads the token from the URL fragment and POSTs it to `/auth/verify`. The token never reaches the server as a query string.

200.description: An HTML page that completes the sign-in client-side.

post.x-aardvark-browser-tryit-reason: This auth endpoint is same-origin to the gateway dashboard, so browser calls from this docs page are blocked by CORS. Use a generated request sample or the dashboard instead.
post.description: The token in the body is the credential. On success sets the `__Host-av_session` cookie. NOTE: this endpoint's responses use the non-standard shape `{ ok, error }` (read by the landing page's script), not the usual error envelope.

token.description: The magic-link token from the URL fragment.

ok.description: `true` when the token was consumed and the `__Host-av_session` cookie was minted; `false` carries an `error` code.
Always `false` in this error response.
Always `false` in this error response.

get.description: Headline metrics over the last `days` (default 30, capped at 365): conversation/answer/feedback counts, verdict mix, uncertainty rate, engagement, support-deflection rate, and top languages.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Window length in days (default 30, capped at 365).

window_days.description: Resolved look-back window in days (default `30`, capped at `365`).

until.description: Window end, epoch-ms (exclusive); the request time.

conversations.description: Distinct conversations with at least one stored turn in the window.

answers.description: Total stored answer turns (transcript rows) in the window.

up.description: Count of thumbs-up votes on answers in the window.

down.description: Count of thumbs-down votes on answers in the window.

satisfaction_rate.description: `up / (up + down)` in `[0,1]`, or `null` when no votes.

feedback.description: Reader thumbs vote tallies and the derived satisfaction rate.

confident.description: Turns classified `confident` by the analysis pass.

unconfident.description: Turns classified `unconfident` by the analysis pass.

not_found.description: Turns classified `not_found` (no answer in the docs).

analyzed.description: Turns with any verdict: sum of the four `verdicts` counts.

escalation_shown.description: Times the escalation/contact link was shown under a downvoted answer.

escalation_click.description: Times a reader clicked the escalation/contact link.

deflection_rate.description: `1 − escalation_click / answers`, clamped `[0,1]`; `null` when no escalations or answers.

language.description: ISO query-language code from the analysis pass.

count.description: Turns analyzed in this language during the window.

languages.description: Top query languages by turn count (max 12, descending).

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.

get.description: Per-type counts and the most recent authoring events over the last 90 days. Returns clean empty shapes when no rows exist.

count.description: Number of events of this `event_type` recorded within the 90-day window.

events.description: Per-`event_type` counts over the last 90 days, busiest type first (≤500 types).

event_type.description: Client-defined authoring event name for this row.

detail.description: Optional free-text payload sent with the event; `null` when omitted.

ts.description: Epoch-ms time the event was recorded.

recent.description: The most recent authoring events, newest first (≤50), within the 90-day window.

total.description: Total events over the last 90 days, summed from the per-type `events` counts.

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.

post.description: Scaffold ingest for a future authoring client. Public-key authed like the other reader beacons; `event_type` is restricted to letters/digits/'.'/'_'/'-'. Best-effort over the per-account daily cap.

ok.description: Always `true`; best-effort ack even when the row is dropped over the daily cap.

error.message: Origin not allowed for this key.

post.description: OpenAI-compatible chat-completions endpoint. The gateway authorizes the key, reserves the input cost against the account balance, forwards the request to the configured upstream model, and streams the response back as Server-Sent Events while metering actual spend. The system prompt is always injected by the gateway; client-supplied system messages are stripped. The sampling fields (temperature/top_p/frequency_penalty/presence_penalty) are clamped to valid ranges.

model.description: Upstream model id to forward to.

messages.description: OpenAI-style message array; must include at least one user message.

stop.description: Up to 4 stop sequences (string or array); excess is truncated.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature forwarded upstream, clamped to `[0, 2]`; dropped if non-numeric or `NaN`.

top_p.description: Nucleus-sampling probability mass forwarded upstream, clamped to `[0, 1]`; dropped if non-numeric.

frequency_penalty.description: Repetition penalty by token frequency, clamped to `[-2, 2]`; dropped if non-numeric.

presence_penalty.description: Penalty for tokens already present, clamped to `[-2, 2]`; dropped if non-numeric.

messages.content: How do I install the CLI?

200.description: A `text/event-stream` of OpenAI-style completion chunks (`data: {…}` lines, terminated by `data: [DONE]`). The optional `X-Stop-Truncated: true` header signals dropped excess `stop` sequences.

error.message: Total attachment payload is too large.
This key isn't allowed from this origin.
Could not determine a price for this model (unknown to OpenRouter, or pricing is temporarily unavailable). The request was not billed.

post.description: Stores a free-text reader comment, optionally tied to a page path and an island-generated survey/question id. Best-effort over the per-account daily cap. `page_url` must be a safe site-relative pathname or it is stored as null.

ok.description: Always `true` on accept; best-effort, so `true` even when the daily cap drops the row.

error.message: Origin not allowed for this key.

get.description: Recent turns grouped into conversations, each with the reader vote and (when available) an automated analysis verdict. `filter=needs_attention` restricts to low-confidence / doc-gap / not-found turns; `verdict`/`intent`/`vote`/`q`/`tag` narrow it further.

parameters.name: verdict
parameters.description: Restrict to a single analysis verdict.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Set to `needs_attention` to restrict the result (default `all`).

parameters.name: intent
parameters.description: Restrict to a single detected intent.

parameters.name: vote
parameters.description: Restrict to `up`/`down` votes.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Free-text search over question/answer.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Restrict to a custom tag.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Epoch-ms lower bound; keeps turns with `ts` >= this (inclusive).

parameters.name: until
parameters.description: Epoch-ms upper bound; keeps turns with `ts` < this (exclusive).

conversations.description: Recent turns grouped by `conversation_id`, newest conversation first, turns ascending within.

truncated.description: `true` when more matching turns exist beyond the 100-turn list cap.

filter.description: Back-compat toggle echo: `needs_attention` when that legacy filter applied, else `all`.

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.

get.description: CSV of the conversation turns matching the same filters as `GET /v1/conversations` (every cell formula-injection-guarded). An `X-Aardvark-Export-Truncated: true` response header signals the export cap was hit.

parameters.name: verdict
parameters.description: Restrict to a single analysis verdict.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Set to `needs_attention` to restrict the result (default `all`).

parameters.name: intent
parameters.description: Restrict to a single detected intent.

parameters.name: vote
parameters.description: Restrict to `up`/`down` votes.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Free-text search over question/answer.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Restrict to a custom tag.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Epoch-ms lower bound; keeps turns with `ts` >= this (inclusive).

parameters.name: until
parameters.description: Epoch-ms upper bound; keeps turns with `ts` < this (exclusive).

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.

get.description: The account's insight-digest opt-in: whether `enabled`, the `cadence`, and when it was `last_sent_ts`.

enabled.description: Whether the account is opted into the periodic emailed insights digest; `false` by default.

cadence.description: Digest frequency, one of `week` or `month`; defaults to `week`.

last_sent_ts.description: Epoch-ms watermark of the last digest send; `0` when never sent.

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.

post.description: Opt the account owner into (or out of) a weekly/monthly emailed insights digest. Owner only. **Owner only.**

ok.description: Always `true`; the digest preferences were saved.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.

post.description: Public-key beacon (migration 0027): the escalation/contact link was SHOWN under a downvoted answer (`escalation_shown`) or CLICKED (`escalation_click`). Decoupled from the transcript and subject to a per-account daily row cap; drives the dashboard's support-deflection metric.

conversation_id.description: Conversation the event belongs to (≤100 chars).

ok.description: Always `true` on accept; a deduped or over-cap beacon still ACKs 200.

error.message: Origin not allowed for this key.

get.description: The owner-facing READ of reader thumbs votes — the capture POST is `POST /v1/feedback`. Returns up/down counts, the satisfaction rate, and recent rated answers. Dashboard-authed (session OR secret key); a public key is rejected at the door.

up.description: Count of thumbs-up votes in the window.

down.description: Count of thumbs-down votes in the window.

up_rate.description: `up / (up + down)` in `[0,1]`, or null when there are no votes.

ts.description: Epoch-ms time the vote was recorded.

conversation_id.description: Conversation the rated answer belongs to.

recent.description: Recent rated answer turns, newest first.

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.

post.description: Stores a reader's vote on a specific answer turn. Best-effort: over the per-account daily cap the vote is silently dropped but still returns 200. Deduplicated on (account, conversation_id, turn).

turn.description: Answer index within the conversation (0–10000); out-of-range coerces to 0.

ok.description: Always `true`; the vote is best-effort, so an over-cap drop still ACKs `200`.

error.message: Origin not allowed for this key.

get.description: One cross-stream snapshot of the account's reader feedback over a recent 30-day window — thumbs, page ratings, survey responses, and conversation sentiment — powering the metric tiles at the top of the dashboard Feedback panel. Each sub-stream degrades to zeros if its backing table isn't present yet.

window_days.description: Look-back window in days for the snapshot (fixed at 30, matching the Insights tab).

since.description: Epoch-ms start of the window (`now - 30 days`).

ratings.description: Page star-rating aggregates over the window; zeros until that stream's table lands.

thumbs.description: Thumbs-vote totals over the window (always present — the `feedback` table ships on `main`).

surveys.description: Structured survey-response aggregates over the window; zeros until that stream's table lands.

sentiment.description: Conversation-sentiment distribution over the window; zeros until that stream's table lands.

recent_activity.description: A small recent-activity feed across the streams for the panel header.

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.

get.description: The static catalog of AI authoring capabilities (e.g. keyword and description generation, the style-guide pass) that can be enabled to run on a connected repo. Readable by any authenticated dashboard user.

capabilities.description: The catalog of available authoring capabilities, each with its id, label, and runner command.

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.
GitHub integration is not configured on this gateway.

post.description: Declarative per-repo automation config: for each capability set whether it's enabled, its schedule (`cron`, floored to at most once per hour), run-on-push, base branch, and project dir. A pure DB apply — no GitHub writes. Owner only. **Owner only.**

automations.description: Array of per-capability automation configs to apply (last-write-wins per capability).

enabled.description: Whether the automation is active after the apply.

run_on_push.description: Whether a matching push fires the automation.

cron.description: Resolved 5-field cron schedule, or null when unscheduled.

base_branch.description: Branch the automation runs against, or null for the repo default.

project_dir.description: Subdirectory the vark project lives in, or null for the repo root.

automations.description: The repo's automation rows after the apply.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.
That repo isn't connected to this account.
This repo's installation is owned by a different account — reconnect it.
GitHub integration is not configured on this gateway.

post.description: Mints a single-use install `state` and returns the GitHub App install URL carrying it. The browser follows that URL; GitHub echoes `state` back to the setup callback, which binds the installation to this account. Owner only. **Owner only.**

install_url.description: GitHub App install URL with the single-use `state` embedded; redirect the browser here to start the install.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.
GITHUB_APP_SLUG is not set, so the install URL can't be built.

get.description: Everything the dashboard's Docs Quality Checks page needs: whether the integration is configured, the install-URL slug, the account's installations, and each connected repo with its per-capability automation config.

configured.description: Whether the gateway is fully configured for the integration (app creds, slug, webhook secret, OAuth creds).

app_slug.description: The GitHub App's URL slug used to build the install URL, or null when unset.

capabilities.description: The static catalog of AI authoring capabilities that can be enabled per repo.

github_login.description: The org/user login the app is installed on.

suspended.description: `true` when GitHub has suspended the installation.

installations.description: GitHub App installations bound to this account.

default_branch.description: Repo default branch (the automation base when none is set).

installation_id.description: Installation that grants access to this repo.

configured.description: `true` when the repo has at least one automation set up.

run_on_push.description: Whether a matching push fires the automation.

cron.description: Schedule (5-field cron, at most hourly), or null.

base_branch.description: Branch the automation runs against, or null for the repo default.

project_dir.description: Subdirectory the vark project lives in, or null for the repo root.

automations.description: Per-capability automation rows configured on the repo.

repos.description: Repos reachable through the installations, each with its automation config.

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.
GitHub integration is not configured on this gateway.

post.description: Stops all automations for a repo by dropping their config rows in one atomic delete. Leaves the app installed (uninstall happens in GitHub) and the repo listed so it can be reconfigured. Owner only. **Owner only.**

disconnected.description: Always `true` once the repo's automations are removed.

repo.description: Echoes the repo that was disconnected.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.
That repo isn't connected to this account.
This repo's installation is owned by a different account.
GitHub integration is not configured on this gateway.

post.description: Dispatches a configured capability immediately on the central runner. The run is recorded as `queued` and advanced by the runner's claim/complete callbacks. A balance or runner-config problem returns 402/503; an already-running capability returns 409. Owner only. **Owner only.**

capability.description: Capability id to run; must be enabled for the repo.

dispatched.description: Always `true` when the run was queued on the central runner.

run_id.description: Server-minted UUID of the queued run, for correlating later run-history rows.

repo.description: Echoes the repo the run was dispatched for.

capability.description: Echoes the capability the run executes.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.
That repo isn't connected to this account.
This repo's installation is no longer connected to this account.
Couldn't start the run on the Aardvark runner — try again.
GitHub integration is not configured on this gateway.

post.description: Cancels the active queued or in-progress run for a repo/capability pair, revokes any already-minted ephemeral inference key, and best-effort cancels the backing GitHub Actions run. Idempotent: idle pairs return `cancelled: false`. **Owner only.**

capability.description: Capability id whose active run should be cancelled.

cancelled.description: `true` when an active run was sealed as cancelled; `false` when no matching run was active.

run_id.description: Server-minted UUID of the cancelled run. Present only when `cancelled` is `true`.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.
GitHub integration is not configured on this gateway.

post.x-aardvark-browser-tryit-reason: This endpoint is server-to-server only, so browser calls from this docs page are blocked by CORS. Use a generated request sample from your backend instead.
post.description: Central-runner callback: the runner claims a queued run and receives an ephemeral inference key plus a repo-scoped GitHub token (minted only once the claim CAS wins). Authed by the runner credential (`RUNNER_CALLBACK_TOKEN` and/or a GitHub Actions OIDC JWT), never a dashboard session.

run_id.description: The gateway run UUID being claimed.

github_run_id.description: The runner's GitHub Actions run id (positive integer), bound to the run for callback correlation.

inference_key.description: Ephemeral, per-run secret inference key — returned ONCE, after the claim CAS wins.

repo_token.description: Short-lived GitHub token scoped to the customer repo (contents + pull-requests write).

command.description: The vark runner command to execute for this capability.

installation_id.description: Installation id whose token grants access to the customer repo.

base_branch.description: Branch to run against, or null for the repo default.

project_dir.description: Subdirectory the vark project lives in, or null for the repo root.

error.message: This run was already claimed or finished.

post.x-aardvark-browser-tryit-reason: This endpoint is server-to-server only, so browser calls from this docs page are blocked by CORS. Use a generated request sample from your backend instead.
post.description: Central-runner callback: reports a finished run. Revokes the run's ephemeral key (stops spend), meters the compute, and records the conclusion plus the PR it opened. Idempotent; a late callback can top up a $0-sealed run. Authed by the runner credential.

run_id.description: The gateway run UUID being completed.

billable_ms.description: Authoritative billable wall-clock for the run, in milliseconds.

conclusion.description: The GitHub Actions conclusion string (e.g. `success`, `failure`); unknown non-empty values normalize to `failure`.

pr_url.description: URL of the PR the run opened in the customer repo (must be an https github.com PR in that repo).

github_run_id.description: The runner's GitHub Actions run id; under OIDC it must match the token's `run_id` claim.

ok.description: Always `true` once the run is finalized (or already was).

already_finalized.description: Present and `true` when the run was already billed — an idempotent no-op.

error.message: Couldn't finalize the run; it will be retried.

get.description: Run history for a connected repo, newest first, optionally filtered by `capability`. Each row carries status, conclusion, the PR the run opened, and the metered compute cost.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: Repo `owner/name` to list runs for (required).

parameters.name: capability
parameters.description: Restrict to one capability's runs.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max rows to return (default 50, capped at 200).

id.description: Stable run UUID (the React table key; `run_number` is null until the run starts).

conclusion.description: GitHub Actions conclusion (e.g. `success`, `failure`), or null until finished.

skip_reason.description: Why a `skipped` run never started (e.g. `insufficient_balance`), or null.

run_number.description: GitHub Actions run number on our runner repo, or null until claimed.

pr_url.description: URL of the PR the run opened in the customer repo, or null.

compute_billed_micro.description: Metered compute cost in micro-USD, or null until finalized.

created_at.description: Epoch-ms time the run was created.

updated_at.description: Epoch-ms time the run row was last updated.

error.message: `repo` query param must be in `owner/name` format.
This credential can't manage the account.
That repo isn't connected to this account.
GitHub integration is not configured on this gateway.

get.x-aardvark-browser-tryit-reason: This endpoint is a browser redirect callback, not a cross-origin API call. Start the GitHub connection flow from the dashboard instead.
get.description: GitHub redirects the browser here after the app is installed or updated. The single-use `state` (minted at connect) recovers which account initiated, an identity check proves the user administers the installation, then it binds the install, syncs repos, and 302-redirects back to the dashboard. Authed by `state`, not a session.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Single-use install state minted at connect; the credential that recovers and binds the initiating account.

parameters.name: installation_id
parameters.description: GitHub's numeric id for the new/updated installation.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: OAuth code GitHub appends so the gateway can prove the user administers this installation.

302.description: Redirect (HTTP 302) to `/dashboard/github?connected=1` on success, or `?error=<code>` otherwise.

Location.description: Dashboard URL to follow, carrying `?connected=1` on success or `?error=<code>` otherwise.

error.message: The gateway origin is misconfigured; contact the operator.

post.x-aardvark-browser-tryit-reason: This endpoint is server-to-server only, so browser calls from this docs page are blocked by CORS. Use a generated request sample from your backend instead.
post.description: GitHub-to-server webhook authenticated by the `X-Hub-Signature-256` HMAC over the raw body. Handles installation lifecycle, repo-selection changes, pushes (run-on-push automations), workflow runs (a backstop for the runner's complete callback), and PRs (PR-url capture). Acks 200 once verified; unrecognized event types are acked and ignored.

schema.description: A GitHub webhook event payload; its shape varies by the `X-GitHub-Event` header.

received.description: Always `true`; acks the verified delivery so GitHub stops retrying.

post.description: Mark a clustered insight (by `label`) as e.g. triaged/resolved with an optional note — drives the dashboard's coverage-gap workflow.

ok.description: Always `true`; the gap's triage status (and note) was recorded.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.

get.description: The cron-clustered analytics surface: live metrics plus Top Questions and Coverage Gaps for the selected period, with prior-period deltas when available.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Rolling window in days.

parameters.name: period_kind
parameters.description: Named period bucket (e.g. `week` / `month`).

parameters.name: period_start
parameters.description: Period start, epoch ms.

window_days.description: Length of the live-metrics window in days; from `?days=`, default `30`, capped at `90`.

bucket_ms.description: Volume-series bucket width in ms: `3600000` (hourly) when window `<=2` days, else `86400000` (daily).

since.description: Inclusive start of the live-metrics window, epoch ms (`now - window_days*day`).

until.description: Exclusive end of the live-metrics window, epoch ms (request time).

generated_ts.description: Epoch-ms write time of the shown cluster snapshot (max row `ts`); null if never clustered.

kind.description: Period granularity: one of `week`, `month`, or `quarter` (`legacy` for pre-versioning rows).

start.description: Inclusive start of the period, epoch ms (week = Monday 00:00 UTC).

end.description: Exclusive end of the period, epoch ms (`0` for `legacy` rows).

period.description: The calendar period this Top Questions / Coverage Gaps snapshot covers; null if never clustered.

kind.description: Period granularity of this history entry: `week`, `month`, or `quarter`.

start.description: Inclusive start of this history period, epoch ms.

end.description: Exclusive end of this history period, epoch ms.

periods.description: Navigable history of clustered periods for this `kind`, newest first.

has_prior.description: True when the immediately prior period has clusters to compute per-cluster trends against.

turns.description: Count of answer turns (transcripts) in the window.

conversations.description: Count of distinct conversations in the window.

up.description: Number of thumbs-up votes in the window.

down.description: Number of thumbs-down votes in the window.

votes.description: Reader thumbs-up/down tallies over the window.

confident.description: Turns classified `confident` by the analysis pass.

unconfident.description: Turns classified `unconfident` by the analysis pass.

not_found.description: Turns classified `not_found` (no answer found) by the analysis pass.

doc_gap.description: Turns classified `doc_gap` (a documentation gap) by the analysis pass.

verdicts.description: Distribution of analysis classifications over turns in the window.

bucket_ts.description: Bucket start, epoch ms, floored to `bucket_ms`.

count.description: Number of turns falling in this bucket.

series.description: Turns-per-time-bucket volume series (sparse; client fills gaps), oldest first.

escalation_shown.description: Times the escalation/contact link was shown under a downvoted answer.

escalation_click.description: Times the escalation/contact link was clicked.

engagement.description: Escalation/contact-link beacon counts driving the support-deflection metric.

count.description: Number of turns in this language within the window.

metrics.description: Live engagement metrics over the `?days=` window (computed on demand, not snapshotted).

label.description: Short human label naming the question theme.

count.description: Approximate number of reader questions in this cluster.

examples.description: Up to 3 representative example questions for this cluster.

top_questions.description: Clustered Top Questions for the period, biggest cluster first.

label.description: Short human label naming the coverage-gap topic.

finding.description: What readers asked and why the assistant struggled (may be null).

recommendation.description: Concrete documentation change that would fix the gap (may be null).

count.description: Approximate number of flagged turns in this cluster.

examples.description: Up to 3 representative example questions for this gap.

status.description: Triage state set via `/v1/insight-status`; defaults to `open` when untouched.

note.description: Optional triage note attached to this gap; null when none.

status_updated_at.description: Epoch-ms time the gap's triage status was last set; null when untouched.

coverage_gaps.description: Clustered Coverage Gaps (flagged turns grouped into doc-gap topics), biggest first.

page.description: Site-relative path of the cited documentation page.

citations.description: Times this page was cited as a source in the window.

downvoted.description: Of those citations, how many sat on a downvoted answer.

pages.description: Most-cited doc pages in the window, busiest first (up to 20).

total.description: Total source-citation events recorded across all pages in the window.

sources.description: Doc-page citation analytics over the metrics window.

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.

post.description: Natural-language Q&A over the account's analytics. Metered against the account balance (it calls the model), so it can return 402/429/502/503 like the chat endpoint.

answer.description: Natural-language reply about the account's analytics; a canned message when no data exists yet.

error.message: No active key to bill this request to.
Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.
Too many questions — try again in a moment.
Couldn't get an answer right now. Please try again.
Couldn't price the assistant model right now. Not billed.

get.description: Returns metadata for every key on the account (prefix, mode, label, allowed origins, revoked flag) — never the hash or secret value.

id.description: Opaque key identifier, used as `:id` in the per-key management routes.

mode.description: Key class: `public` (origin-gateable reader key) or `secret` (account master credential).

key_prefix.description: Short non-secret prefix of the key value, shown to identify the key.

label.description: Human-set display nickname, or `null` if unnamed.

allowed_origins.description: Origin allowlist for a `public` key (`null` means any origin); always `null` for secret keys.

revoked.description: Whether the key has been revoked and can no longer authenticate.

created_at.description: Epoch-ms time the key was minted.

keys.description: Every API key on the account, ordered by `mode` then `created_at`; metadata only.

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.

post.description: Creates a public or secret key. The full key value is returned ONCE. Secret keys are owner-only and capped at one active per account (rotate to replace). Public keys may be origin-scoped.

allowed_origins.description: Allowed origins for a public key; null/omitted = any origin.

key_id.description: UUID row id of the newly minted key, for later rotate/revoke/update calls.

api_key.description: The full key value — shown ONCE.

key_prefix.description: First 20 chars of the key (`aardvark_live_` or `aardvark_secret_` plus a slice), shown for identification.

mode.description: Key class: `public` (origin-gated, site-embedded) or `secret` (full account/CLI credential).

label.description: Display nickname stored for the key; defaults to `aardvark <mode>` when none given.

warnings.description: Advisory notices, e.g. an unscoped public key accepting any origin or a risky wildcard origin.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.
This account already has an active secret key — rotate it instead of minting a second one.

post.description: Updates a key's label and/or a public key's allowed origins. At least one field is required; an omitted field is left unchanged. Non-owners may only update keys they created.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Identifier of the key to update.

label.description: New label (≤64 chars); omit to leave unchanged.

allowed_origins.description: New origin list for a public key; omit to leave unchanged, null to open to all.
Origin allowlist now set on the public key; `null` means any origin is accepted.

warnings.description: Non-fatal advisories, e.g. unrestricted-origin or shared-wildcard cautions about the new config.

label.description: New display nickname after the rename; `null` when the label was cleared or omitted.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.
No such key on this account.

post.description: Permanently revokes a PUBLIC key. Secret keys cannot be bare-revoked (rotate them instead) to avoid locking the owner out.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Identifier of the public key to revoke.

revoked.description: Always `true` on success; a missing or non-public key returns a 404 error instead.

key_id.description: Id of the public key that was revoked, echoing the path `{id}`.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.
No such key on this account.

post.description: Revokes the key and mints a same-mode replacement atomically. The new value is shown once. Non-owners may only rotate keys they created; a demoted member cannot rotate the account secret key.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Identifier of the key to rotate.

key_id.description: Id of the freshly minted replacement key (a new UUID).

api_key.description: The full replacement key value — shown ONCE.

key_prefix.description: Display prefix of the new key value (`mode`-dependent: public vs secret prefix).

mode.description: Mode carried over to the replacement: `public` or `secret`.

rotated_from.description: Id of the now-revoked original key this replacement supersedes.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.
No such key on this account.

get.description: Top tools, recent calls, and a bucketed time series over a window of `days` (default 7, max 90). Buckets are hourly for ≤2 days, daily otherwise.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Window in days (1–90).

window_days.description: Resolved look-back window in days (`?days=`, default `7`, capped at `90`).

bucket_ms.description: Time-series bucket width in ms: hourly (`3600000`) for windows ≤2 days, else daily (`86400000`).

since.description: Epoch-ms start of the window (inclusive); `until` minus `window_days`.

until.description: Epoch-ms end of the window (exclusive); the request time `now`.

tool.description: MCP tool name as beaconed by the docs MCP server.

count.description: Number of calls to this tool within the window.

tools.description: Per-tool call counts in the window, ordered by `count` descending then tool name.

tool.description: MCP tool name for this individual call.

args_summary.description: Short human-readable summary of the call's arguments; `null` when none was recorded.

ts.description: Epoch-ms time the tool call was recorded.

recent.description: Most recent individual tool calls, newest first, up to 100.

bucket_ts.description: Epoch-ms floored start of the bucket (`ts` floored to `bucket_ms`).

count.description: Number of tool calls falling in this bucket.

series.description: Sparse per-bucket call counts over the window; only non-empty buckets, ascending by time.

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.

post.description: The docs MCP server posts one beacon per tool call. A short args summary is either supplied (`args_summary`) or derived from `args`. Server-side callers (no Origin header) bypass the origin allowlist; browsers must pass it. Best-effort over the per-account daily cap.

args.description: Raw args; summarized when args_summary is absent.

ok.description: Always `true` on a 200; the beacon was accepted (also when silently dropped over the daily cap).

error.message: Origin not allowed for this key.

get.description: The dashboard READ of reader star ratings: the overall average, total count, and a per-page breakdown. Dashboard-authed like `/v1/insights`.

overall_avg.description: Mean star rating across all pages, or null when there are no ratings.

pages.description: Per-page rating rollups (path, average, count), busiest first.

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.

post.description: Sets the auto-top-up on/off state, charge amount, and low-balance trigger. At least one field is required; the trigger must be below the charge amount. Enabling requires a saved card and an amount. Owner/admin only.

enabled.description: Enable/disable auto top-up; omit to leave unchanged.

amount_usd.description: Charge amount per top-up (≥ min, whole cents).

low_balance_usd.description: Balance threshold that triggers a top-up (> 0).

auto_topup_enabled.description: Post-write on/off state of auto top-up after this update.

auto_topup_amount_usd.description: Amount charged per auto top-up, in USD; `null` when none is stored.

low_balance_usd.description: Low-balance trigger threshold in USD that arms an auto top-up.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.
Too many settings updates. Please wait a moment.
Card payments are not enabled on this gateway.

post.description: Creates (if needed) a Stripe customer and a hosted SetupIntent Checkout Session, returning the redirect URL. No body required. Owner/admin only.

url.description: Redirect URL of the hosted Stripe SetupIntent Checkout Session for card entry.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.
Too many card-setup attempts. Please wait a moment.
Could not start card setup. Please try again.
Card payments are not enabled on this gateway.

delete.description: Clears the card on file (also disabling auto-top-up) and best-effort detaches it at Stripe. Idempotent; allowed even when card payments are disabled. Owner/admin only.

removed.description: Always `true`; confirms the card on file was cleared and auto-top-up disabled.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.
Too many card-removal attempts. Please wait a moment.

post.description: Server-side re-reads the completed Checkout Session, verifies it belongs to this account, and stores the card on file. Idempotent on a given session_id. Owner/admin only.

session_id.description: The Stripe Checkout Session id from the redirect.

brand.description: Card network brand (e.g. `visa`, `mastercard`); `null` if Stripe omitted it.

last4.description: Last four digits of the saved card; `null` if unavailable.

exp_year.description: Card expiry year, four-digit; `null` if unavailable.

card.description: Non-sensitive details of the card now saved on file, for display.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.
Too many card-setup attempts. Please wait a moment.
Could not verify the card. Please try again.
Card payments are not enabled on this gateway.

post.description: Immediately charges the saved card for `amount_usd` and credits the balance. Use a client `idempotency_key` to make retries safe. A pending charge returns `{ pending: true }` and is credited by the webhook. Owner/admin only.

amount_usd.description: Amount to charge (≥ min, whole cents).

pending.description: Present when the charge is still settling.

balance_usd.description: New balance after the credit (on a synchronous charge).

error.message: amount_usd must be a number of at least $….
Your account isn't active. Reactivate it before topping up.
A charge is already in progress for this account. Please wait a moment and try again.
Too many top-up attempts. Please wait a moment.
Card payments are not enabled on this gateway.

post.description: Public-key capture of a reader star rating for a page (migration 0036), optionally with a comment. Best-effort over the per-account daily cap.

page_url.description: Site-relative pathname the rating is for.

ok.description: Always `true` on accept; best-effort, so `true` even when the daily cap drops the row.

error.message: Origin not allowed for this key.

post.description: Lifts the gateway's automatic cost-unavailable suspension. Operator-set holds and closed accounts cannot be reactivated here. Idempotent. Owner/admin only.

status.description: Account status after the call; `active` on every success path.

changed.description: `true` if this call lifted the pause; `false` when already `active` (no-op).

error.message: This account is closed and can't be reactivated from the dashboard. Contact the gateway operator.
Temporary error reading account state. Please retry.

get.description: The positive/neutral/negative distribution of analyzed conversations over a recent window, plus recent example messages, for the reader-feedback panel. Dashboard-authed like `/v1/insights`.

positive.description: Count of conversations the analysis pass classified positive.

recent_positive.description: A few recent example messages classified positive.

recent_negative.description: A few recent example messages classified negative.

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.

post.x-aardvark-browser-tryit-reason: This endpoint is server-to-server only, so browser calls from this docs page are blocked by CORS. Use a generated request sample from your backend instead.
post.description: Stripe-to-server webhook authenticated by the `Stripe-Signature` header (HMAC over the raw body). Handles `payment_intent.succeeded` (durable credit) and `payment_intent.payment_failed` (audit + auto-top-up streak). Always acks 200 once verified, to stop Stripe's retry storm.

received.description: Always `true`; acks the verified delivery so Stripe stops its retry storm.

error.message: Could not apply the credit; will retry.

get.description: Lists tickets for the account. Plain members see only their own; owners/admins/secret-key callers see the whole account. Capped at 100 (newest first).

message.description: Submitter's free-text message, capped at 4000 characters.

status.description: Triage state; defaults to `open` at creation.

ts.description: Epoch-ms time the ticket was submitted.

items.description: Support tickets for the account, newest first, capped at 100.

truncated.description: `true` when more than 100 tickets exist beyond those returned.

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.

post.description: Files a ticket to the gateway operator. Fail-hard over the per-account daily cap (429). Sends a best-effort operator email; the stored row is the source of truth.

example.message: The usage chart is blank on Safari.

ok.description: Always `true` once the ticket row is stored.

id.description: Server-minted UUID of the stored support ticket.

status.description: Initial triage status of a new ticket; always `open`.

ts.description: Epoch-ms time the ticket was stored.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.
Daily feedback submission limit reached. Please try again later.

post.description: Public-key capture of a structured reader survey answer (migration 0037) — a single/multi choice or a rating — that the Survey island previously sent only to analytics. Best-effort over the per-account daily cap.

answer_value.description: The chosen option(s) or text answer (a JSON array string for `multi`).

rating_value.description: Numeric value for a `rating` question; ignored for other types.

page_url.description: Site-relative pathname the survey was answered on.

ok.description: Always `true` on accept; best-effort, so `true` even when the daily cap drops the row.

error.message: Origin not allowed for this key.

get.description: The dashboard READ of structured survey answers: per-question aggregates (choice bars + rating averages) plus recent open-ended survey comments. Dashboard-authed like `/v1/usage`.

total.description: Number of respondents to the question.

rating_count.description: Number of rating answers folded into `rating_avg`.

count.description: How many respondents picked this option.

choices.description: Per-option counts for a choice question, most-picked first.

rating_avg.description: Mean of the rating answers, or null for a non-rating question / no ratings.

ts.description: Epoch-ms time the comment was submitted.

page_url.description: Site-relative path the comment was left on, or null.

comment.description: Reader-supplied open-ended comment text (escaped before display).

comments.description: Recent open-ended survey comments (the survey-scoped `page_comments` rows).

truncated.description: `true` when more comments exist beyond the returned cap.

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.

get.description: The account's custom tag vocabulary (`tags`) and the per-account cap (`max`).

name.description: Display label of the tag, unique per account; no semicolons or control characters.

description.description: Optional guidance steering how the analysis pass applies this tag; `null` if unset.

ts.description: Epoch-ms time the tag was created.

uses.description: Count of conversations currently labeled with this tag.

tags.description: The account's active (non-deleted) custom tags, sorted alphabetically by `name`.

max.description: Maximum active tags allowed per account (default `20`).

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.

post.description: Add a custom tag to the account vocabulary. Owner/admin only; 409 on a duplicate name or when the per-account cap is reached. **Owner only.**

ok.description: Always `true` once the tag is created and added to the account vocabulary.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.
A tag with that name already exists.

post.description: Edit a tag's description and/or soft-delete it (`deleted: true` hides it from the vocabulary without dropping the labels already applied to conversations). Owner/admin only; at least one of `description`/`deleted` must be supplied, and `404` if no tag matches the id. **Owner only.**

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Tag id — the trailing path segment after `/v1/tags/` (≤100 chars).

description.description: New human description for the tag; `null` clears it.

ok.description: Always `true` once the update (or soft-delete) is applied.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.

get.description: Returns the account's members. Owners/admins also see pending invites; plain members see members only.

user_id.description: Stable id of the member's user record.

email.description: Member's login email, joined from the `users` table.

role.description: Member's tier on the account: `owner`, `admin`, or `member`.

status.description: Membership state; always `active` here (invited/removed rows are excluded).

created_at.description: Epoch-ms time the membership was created (account join).

members.description: Active members on the shared billing account, oldest membership (the owner) first.

email.description: Email the invite was sent to (not yet accepted).

role.description: Role the invite will grant on accept: `member` or `admin`.

expires_at.description: Epoch-ms time the invite link expires (~7 days after issue).

created_at.description: Epoch-ms time the invite was issued; newest invites listed first.

invites.description: Pending unconsumed invites; present only for `owner`/`admin` callers, else empty.

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.

post.description: Owner/admin only. Emails a magic invite link (valid ~7 days). Owners may invite admins; admins may invite members only. Invites never grant owner. Neutral 200 regardless of delivery.

ok.description: Always `true`; the neutral success acknowledgment (returned regardless of email delivery).

email.description: Normalized email address the invite link was sent to.

role.description: Role the invitee will hold on accepting; `member` or `admin` (never `owner`).

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.
Too many invites. Please wait a moment and try again.
Team invites are disabled on deploy previews (the roster is forked production data).

post.description: Owner/admin only. Cancels an unconsumed invite for an email. Idempotent (revoked: 0 when none pending).

ok.description: Always `true` once the request validates; the cancel ran (even if nothing matched).

email.description: Normalized invitee email whose pending invite was targeted, echoed back.

revoked.description: Count of pending (unconsumed) invite tokens deleted; `0` when none were pending.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.

post.description: Owner/admin only, with a last-owner guard. Admins may remove non-owners. Removal revokes the member's keys and deletes their invites first.

ok.description: `true` once the member is removed and their keys/invites revoked.

user_id.description: Echoes back the removed member's user id.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.
No such member on this account.

post.description: Owner/admin only, with a last-owner guard. Non-owners cannot grant or alter owner/admin. Demotions revoke the member's keys before the change.

ok.description: Always `true` when the role change was applied successfully.

user_id.description: Identifier of the member whose role was changed.

error.message: Missing the dashboard request header on a state-changing request.
No such member on this account.

get.description: A flat, cursor-paged feed of answer turns in ascending `(ts, conversation_id, turn)` order (oldest first) — built for forward incremental sync. Page with `next_cursor` / `has_more`.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Cursor: return turns at or after this epoch-ms timestamp (inclusive lower bound), or pass a prior page's `next_cursor`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max turns to return.

conversation_id.description: Conversation this turn belongs to; the reader-island session/thread id.

turn.description: Zero-based turn index within the conversation.

question.description: The reader's question text for this turn; null if not stored.

answer.description: The assistant's answer text for this turn; null if not stored.

reasoning.description: Full model reasoning for the turn (not truncated); null if absent.

ts.description: Epoch-ms time the transcript turn was recorded; the pagination sort key.

vote.description: Reader feedback: `up`, `down`, or null when no vote was cast.

classification.description: Analysis verdict: `confident`, `unconfident`, `not_found`, or `doc_gap`; null until analyzed.

summary.description: One-sentence analysis summary of the turn; null until analyzed.

language.description: Reader question's language as a short ISO code (e.g. `en`, `ja`, `pt-BR`); null if unknown.

turns.description: Page of recent answer turns, ascending by the `(ts, conversation_id, turn)` sort key.

count.description: Number of turns returned on this page.

next_cursor.description: Opaque continuation token to pass as `since`; null when no further page remains.

has_more.description: True when another non-empty page exists beyond this one.

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.

post.description: Upserts one conversation turn (question + answer, optionally the model's reasoning) keyed on (key, conversation_id, turn). At least one of question/answer is required. Best-effort over the per-account daily cap; an out-of-range `turn` is rejected rather than coerced.

question.description: The user's question (≤2000 chars); at least one of question/answer is required.

answer.description: The model's answer (≤16000 chars); at least one of question/answer is required.

sources.description: Doc-page URLs this answer cited; deduped, capped at 20 per turn, fanned out to `source_events`.

example.question: How do I build my site?

ok.description: Always `true`; returned on a stored turn or a best-effort ack over the daily cap.

error.message: Origin not allowed for this key.

get.description: Returns the account's stored transcripts grouped into conversations (newest first), each turn carrying its vote. Capped at ~300 turns; `truncated` signals older history exists.

conversations.description: Stored conversations, newest first; each holds its turns (`turn`, `question`, `answer`, `vote`) ascending.

truncated.description: `true` when older turns exist beyond the ~300-turn cap and were omitted.

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.

get.description: The dashboard overview payload: account status, balance, recent usage rows, top-ups, reader feedback/comments, account events, and the payment/auto-top-up configuration. Readable even when the account is suspended.

owner_email.description: Billing-account owner's email from the `accounts` row (not a member's address).

status.description: Account state, e.g. `active`, `suspended`, `closed`, or `unknown` if the row is missing.

reason.description: Why the account was paused: `cost_unavailable`, `operator`, or `self_serve`.

ts.description: Epoch-ms time the suspension was recorded.

request_id.description: Request id that triggered the suspension, for support correlation.

self_serviceable.description: Whether the dashboard Reactivate button applies; true only for a `cost_unavailable` pause.

suspension.description: Active-suspension summary when `status` is `suspended`; otherwise null.

account.description: Account identity and current standing block.

reserved_usd.description: Funds reserved against in-flight requests, in USD.

balance.description: Current balance snapshot from the Durable Object, in USD.

ts.description: Epoch-ms time the usage row was written.

model.description: Upstream model slug billed for the request.

prompt_tokens.description: Input token count for the request.

completion_tokens.description: Output token count for the request.

openrouter_usd.description: Published OpenRouter list cost in USD (pre-markup; not the operator's backend cost).

billed_usd.description: Amount charged to the account in USD (list cost times markup).

generation_id.description: Upstream generation id used to reconcile the final cost.

cost_source.description: How the cost was derived: `inline`, `generation_api`, or `provisional`.

usage.description: Up to 50 most recent metered-request ledger rows, newest first.

ts.description: Epoch-ms time the top-up was credited.

source.description: Origin of the credit, e.g. `stripe`, `auto_topup`, or an operator grant.

note.description: Optional free-text note attached to the credit.

topups.description: Up to 20 most recent balance credits, newest first. Present only for owner/admin callers; omitted for members.

ts.description: Epoch-ms time the vote was recorded.

conversation_id.description: Conversation the rated answer belongs to.

question.description: The reader question that the rated answer addressed.

feedback.description: Up to 50 most recent reader thumbs votes, newest first.

ts.description: Epoch-ms time the comment was submitted.

page_url.description: Site-relative path the comment was left on, or null.

comment.description: Reader-supplied open-ended comment text (escaped before display).

comments.description: Up to 50 most recent reader page comments, newest first.

ts.description: Epoch-ms time the event was recorded.

request_id.description: Request id correlated with the event, if any.

events.description: Up to 20 most recent account decision-log entries, newest first.

stripe_enabled.description: Whether the gateway has a Stripe secret key and can accept card payments at all.

has_card.description: Whether a card is saved on file for the account.

last4.description: Last four digits of the saved card.

card.description: Display-only details of the saved card, or null when none.

auto_topup_enabled.description: Whether auto top-up is turned on.

auto_topup_amount_usd.description: Amount charged per auto top-up in USD, or null when unset.

auto_topup_failures.description: Consecutive auto-top-up charge failures since the last success.

auto_topup_last_error.description: Message from the most recent auto-top-up failure, or null.

low_balance_usd.description: Available-balance threshold in USD that triggers an auto top-up.

min_usd.description: Minimum allowed auto-top-up charge amount in USD.

max_usd.description: Maximum allowed top-up amount in USD (Stripe per-charge technical limit).

payment.description: Card-on-file and auto-top-up configuration for the Billing section. Present only for owner/admin callers; omitted for members.

ts.description: Epoch-ms time the payment event was recorded.

type.description: Event type, e.g. card saved/removed or a charge succeeded/declined.

amount_usd.description: Charge amount in USD, or null for non-charge events.

detail.description: Free-text detail about the event, e.g. a decline reason.

payment_events.description: Up to 20 most recent billing-activity entries (card lifecycle, declined charges), newest first. Present only for owner/admin callers; omitted for members.

error.message: This credential can't manage the account.

get.summary: Billing usage by member and account key
get.description: Owner/admin billing usage grouped by the team member who minted each key, over an optional [since, until) window. Account-owned secret/admin/CLI usage is listed as one row per key with safe key metadata.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Inclusive lower bound, epoch ms (default: 30 days ago).

parameters.name: until
parameters.description: Exclusive upper bound, epoch ms (default: now).

since.description: Inclusive start of the resolved `[since, until)` window, epoch-ms.

until.description: Exclusive end of the resolved `[since, until)` window, epoch-ms (clamped to now).

user_id.description: User who minted the keys, or `null` for an account-owned key row.

email.description: Member's email, or `null` for an account-owned key row or a since-deleted user.

key_id.description: Id of the account-owned key this row aggregates, or `null` for a member-attributed row.

key_prefix.description: Non-secret prefix of the account-owned key, or `null` for a member-attributed row.

key_label.description: Display label of the account-owned key, or `null` for a member-attributed row.

key_mode.description: `public` or `secret` for an account-owned key row, or `null` for a member-attributed row.

is_account_key.description: `true` when this row aggregates an account-owned key (`user_id` is `null`) rather than a member.

label.description: Display name: key label/prefix for account-owned key rows, else the member email, else `(member <id8>)` for a deleted user.

requests.description: Number of usage-ledger rows (requests) attributed to this member or account-owned key.

prompt_tokens.description: Summed prompt (input) tokens across this row's requests.

completion_tokens.description: Summed completion (output) tokens across this row's requests.

total_tokens.description: Sum of `prompt_tokens` and `completion_tokens` for this row.

billed_usd.description: Total amount billed to this row over the window, in USD.

members.description: Usage groups, sorted by spend descending with member rows before account-owned key rows on ties.

requests.description: Total requests across all rows over the window.

prompt_tokens.description: Total prompt (input) tokens across all rows.

completion_tokens.description: Total completion (output) tokens across all rows.

total_tokens.description: Sum of `prompt_tokens` and `completion_tokens` across all rows.

billed_usd.description: Total amount billed across all rows over the window, in USD (summed in micro-USD, converted once).

totals.description: Grand-total summary across all rows for the window.

error.message: Billing usage is available to owners and admins only.

Error.description: Standard JSON error envelope shared by all endpoints (except POST /auth/verify, which returns `{ ok: false, error }`).

error.description: Error detail object — a stable machine `code` plus a human-readable `message`.

code.description: Stable machine-readable error code (e.g. `invalid_key`, `insufficient_balance`); safe to switch on.

message.description: Human-readable explanation of what went wrong; for display/logging, not for branching.

publicKey.description: Public widget key, `Authorization: Bearer aardvark_live_…`. Origin-scoped.

secretKey.description: Secret/CLI key, `Authorization: Bearer aardvark_secret_…`. Resolves as account owner.

sessionCookie.name: __Host-av_session
sessionCookie.description: Dashboard session cookie from the magic-link flow (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Lax).

csrfHeader.name: X-Aardvark-Dashboard
csrfHeader.description: Non-empty CSRF header required on session-authenticated mutations (secret-key auth is exempt).

stripeSignature.name: Stripe-Signature
stripeSignature.description: Stripe webhook HMAC signature over the raw request body.

githubSignature.name: X-Hub-Signature-256
githubSignature.description: GitHub webhook HMAC signature (`sha256=…`) over the raw request body, verified against `GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. Server-to-server only.

runnerToken.description: Aardvark central-runner credential — the shared `RUNNER_CALLBACK_TOKEN` bearer and/or a GitHub Actions OIDC JWT (selected by `RUNNER_AUTH_MODE`). Server-to-server only; never a dashboard session or API key.

githubSetupState.name: state
githubSetupState.description: Single-use install `state` minted at connect and echoed back by GitHub to the setup callback — binds the install to the account that initiated it (CSRF + anti-hijack).
